channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low. — 86 replies — 344.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low.

Open thread • Replies: 86 • Updated: 2026-06-04 22:32 UTC

OP

At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low. Logically thinking there would be at least SOME buying pressure from other people in the know and price would be held up at key points with traces of bullish absorption in the chart yet theres none of that just a fast move up into a slow bleed. If you do any type of chart watching you would know exactly what im talking about. With just a glance at the LINK chart you can tell there is not a single good/bullish thing about it.

You can't convince me people involved in these announcements with chainlink wouldn't buy some themselves and talk to their other high-class or wealthy friends and those people would buy and word would continue to spread. It just doesnt make sense unless.... the people with inside knowledge know LINK isnt necessary for the services chainlink provides.

Call me a faggot call me indian call me fudder call me retard but it just doesnt make ANY sense despite all of these announcements and the apparent fact that these giant companies are using chainlink that the LINK does not trend upward.

I dont know why im posting this I guess I just wanted to get some thoughts from you guys or rant into the void. Ive been in since 2021 and bought around 15 dollars. I'm not sure if I can hold much longer and on the other hand I dont know what I would even buy even if I sold. I wish I could invest in the company Chainlink.

Early replies (first 3)

62312631
How big is your stack

62312653
>LINK starts to moon in sats
>fuddies get riled up
Like clockwork

62312685
>>62312653
ironically this is exactly how they get away with their accumulation. either during btc dips or OTC buys. meanwhile retail looks at the price, demoralization continues, they continue to accumulate.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62316443
>>62316370
>at first he claimed that LINK did pump
After the DTCC news? Where did I say that? Quote it.

BTC had already been busy keeping Link under $10 for some time when DTCC dropped, but Link did make an attempt at climbing to $11 in the hours immediately after the DTCC announcement. Bitcoin dropped below $80k when Link got to $10.8.

62316444
>>62312614
>At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low
It's not a probability. Chainlink network must use the LINK token to secure the network

>>62316277
bc stellar was about 15 cents and people buy those like lottery tickets

62316462
>>62314036
they are buying like 0.5% of the supply (5M tokens) per year with the reserve currently, but they release ~7% of the circulating supply per year (70M tokens)

the unlocks vastly outpace the reserve buyback so your logic doesn't really make sense

62316472
>>62316462
6.5% issuance is still lower than other projects

62316510
>>62316405
>failing basic reading comprehension, since I never claimed news pump prices
If only you were smart enough to realize just how hilariously ironic that is.

Anyway, pls answer this now: >>62316277

62316518
>>62316444
binance ripped out the useless token from their chainlink copy and replaced it with the native coin. works great, forcing customers to go trough a shitcoin is not an advantage. they just sell it as soon as they can, the only thing it generates is annoyance

62316569
>>62316518
>binance ripped out the useless token from their chainlink copy and replaced it with the native coin
The only reason they were able to do that is because all of the oracle nodes were Binance itself.
Also Binance is shutting down Binance Oracle. They're switching to an even more centralized one that they also own.

62316605
>>62313954
>and we don't know where that money is going, add to that the fucking build program, where a project gives em a share of their shitcoin and they also subsidize the network for them using the preminted tokens.

The BUILD program is not the stakers. Let me repeat, the BUILD program is not for the stakers. It is for developers, startups. Not every startup can afford to pay oracle costs. This program is a way to grow these companies, the actual users of the network and get them up and running and profitable which would drive more usage of the Chainlink network which should drive the value up. Treat it as Chainlink's own VC fund they're sharing with you through the current community staking pool.

>>62313866
>here is the simple reason why we don't pump:
>the fucking SCALE PROGRAM.
SCALE falls into the same regard, chains pay what they have to get in line so Chainlink can deploy their services to the chain, which attracts developers to deploy their Chainlink integrated dapps which gain more usage.


You can make the case that SCALE chains payments should be converted to Link from the market. The bigger issue really is that a lot of Chainlink's services in general are paid in fiat and other currencies. For instance the price feeds are paid for in USD and Chainlink pays the nodes out of pocket from their own team wallets. This disassociates the link token and consequently decouples the link price from the usage of the network.

I get you are saying similar things but you're applying undue criticism where it doesn't belong. Chainlink doing chains a favor and subsidizing costs through the SCALE program to get the Chainlink network deployed and up and running on their chains is fine as it should theoretically lead to more usage of the network. But when the usage of the network isn't reflected in the token, that's the issue. And staking isn't out yet. This 0.2 version we have is just charity work for hardcore investors and the Reserve is a dumb marketing move.

62316641
>>62316569
yeah, chainlink itself kinda sucks, even without the token

62316645
>>62316518
>binance ripped out the useless token from their chainlink copy and replaced it with the native coin
binance also ran their blockchain on about five nodes when it first launched, they don't care about decentralized economic security. when you use bainance chain you trust binance, and that's fine for shitcoins, but no serious enterprise is going to run a billion dollar tokenization project on that (unless they have a sweet deal with binance)

62316655
>>62316605
>For instance the price feeds are paid for in USD and Chainlink pays the nodes out of pocket from their own team wallets.
98% of Bitcoin mining rewards come from Satoshi's uncirculating supply.
The absolute vast majority of cryptos have a similar "incentivisation" scheme.

You are clueles a clueless ape.

>>62316641
Tell it to DTCC

62316724
>>62316605
>The BUILD program is not the stakers
Yeah, I get it. I know. But my point is that BUILD is also one of those initiatives that effectively dilutes us and further dissociates the token from the network.

To me, all of these initiatives look more like ways for CLL to monetize or offload their pre-minted tokens. The SCALE program brings in millions of dollars to CLL, and those millions are effectively paid for with the pre-minted tokens Sergey owns. Basically, he's doing OTC deals through private agreements by selling locked tokens.

There are many arrangements where CLL pays with locked tokens, and those deals result in tokens that aren't even circulating being used instead of requiring actual market demand. This reduces the need for clients to acquire LINK through the open market and prevents organic token usage driven by the Chainlink network itself.

It's weird, but don't you see it? CLL basically uses its locked tokens to make the Chainlink network function while simultaneously receiving massive checks.

Also, I didn't hear you address the GMX fees. 1.2% of GMX's yearly fees is a huge amount of money. Where is that revenue going?

Again, it's another example of a deal where pre-minted tokens are exchanged to subsidize the network for CLL's clients. That's why we don't pump. Sergey owns a massive stack of LINK, and he's monetizing it while preventing clients from having to buy LINK on the open market to use the Chainlink network.

In the end, the LINK token feels like a bagholder token. It's essentially "minted" by Sergey, used by the network, and then eventually sold on the open market. The result is a one-way flow where tokens move from Sergey's wallets, through the Chainlink ecosystem, and into the market. Meanwhile, the tokens already circulating in the market aren't actually being used by anyone.

62316776
>>62316724
The entire point of the way its being done now is no different than a drug dealer, they're trying to get everyone hooked so they're dependent, and then the free samples go bye bye. The question is, will these entities actually be hooked. Chainlink is counting on the overall growth of the ecosystem, so they can eventually turn off the faucet and get all of that turned into link tokens.

The big issue is its a chicken and egg problem, you need enough institutional demand for people to need to use blockchain services along with people who actually care about security (binance doesn't give two shits if oracles fuck up because they just want your money), but in order for those institutional players to want to use those services, they expect you to already have that level of security in place via staking. So chainlink is trying to complete that loop by funding the initial part themselves.

Now its a waiting game, either CLL turns off the faucet and starts cashing in (probably have to at least wait until things like the DTCC and Swift roll out whatever they're using with chainlink for that to work), or this goes on for a few more years until the token runs out and the project fails. If it were just a blatant grift though, the way they're running it is highly inefficient and they could extract a lot more capital, which tells you they want to succeed (why wouldn't they, if they do they revolutionize global finance), they just might run out of runway. Unfortunately if they run out of runway the token holders are going to be the ones trapped on the plane as it bursts into flames, but that's just the reality of risk/reward investing. If there were no risk, why would there be any reward?

62316802
>>62312690
>whynotboth.jpg

62316850
>>62316776
too bad you didn't get the trips, but i totally agree with you and that's why i've been bagholding almost 9 years, in the end it always comes down to do we trust sergey? and the answer is yes, i mean just look like that scammer charles i don't even know how ADA got such mcap with such a scammy fucking "muh ceo". i trust sergey, but what kinda pisses me off is why isn't he giving us more than 4% ffs, even my bank lady is telling me she could get me 4.7% on a safe "life insurance" account, ffs chainlink is a risky bet that isn't incentivizing us enough, imo the yield on staking should be around 8% knowing the risks we are taking bagholding this shitcoin, the rewards would go from 15M$ to 30M$ which isn't a stretch knowing they make hundreds of millions, right now my only source of income (i stopped the wage like 2 years ago) is chainlink rewards and build rewards, i have no other source of income and i'm watching my stack melt while my "muh rewards" also melting it kinda pisses me off, but that's me.

in the end i know once DTCC and swift go live the rewards will increase exponentially, I just hope sergey isn't going to tell us, sorry fags im just gonna increase the reserve, without sharing some of the generated network fees with the stakers

62316865
there's a pepe in Chainlink content

62316876
>>62316850
I mean yeah but the current "staking" is just gibs, if you up that you're shortening the runway to make all this work. And even then its not just trusting Sergey, its trusting that there aren't bigger fish trying to ensure CLL runs out of runway so they can swoop into the wreckage with their own solution. That's my biggest fear, that some other entity knows they can't compete on the current playing field, so they just draw out the timeline, via shorting the token and other financial games, to the point CLL can't do it, then in 5 years they've built their own. The reason nobody can compete with chainlink now is network effect, but if you destroy the current landscape and start from zero that doesn't matter.

Then again though, the other reason to believe in link is they have their own huge insitutional backers (Swift, DTCC) that may look to ensure this can't be stopped. At the end of the day though we are just guppies hoping to ride the right whale. Its why I never tell anyone to buy link, I know its a massive risk and I'm not going to be the one who takes the blame if it doesn't work out. I don't bring up crypto at all anymore. And the one bullish thing I can say, IRL, nobody I know discusses crypto anymore either (and I've never met anyone who knows anything about link either, if they do talk crypto at all).

Also to your last sentence, not necessarily. Proper staking will depend on price, if the price stays low the rewards will shoot up until it levels off, if the price moons the rewards will be small, potentially much smaller than current staking (but price will be so high you'll be able to sell off a small portion of your stack and not care).

62316897
>>62316850
also anon you really should get another source of income, if there are bigger players trying to drag this out, and some of them want to do so for accumulation purposes, situations like yours are exactly what they're doing it for, because they know people will get comfortable thinking "surely it has to happen soon" and start living off their stack, selling directly to their wallets.

Not shilling at all, but just saying, don't let them win if you actually believe in your investment.

62316939
>>62316876
> its trusting that there aren't bigger fish trying to ensure CLL runs out of runway so they can swoop into the wreckage with their own solution
i never thought of this, the factthat CLL relies so muc on their preminted token actually makes them vulnerable, that's a very interesting POV i never thought of, thanks so much man, so the price suppression is to prevent CLL from achieving their goals, ...woahhhh, this opens up a whole new world of thinking in my head ffs this might be why BTC shits the bed every time link tries to moon or gets a news. this is actually crazy.

>the other reason to believe in link is they have their own huge insitutional backers
this! yeah i agree with that especially knowing that sergey was a nobody russian young lad, but he was shaking hands with the biggest, how come schwab back then mentioned smartcontract.com when they were nobodies? also all that intimacy with SWIFT, makes me think that chainlink can't be replaced atleast in the eyes of financial institutions, there is a strong lobby pushing chainlink sergey wasn't picked by chance, there is some shit we don't actually know.

> we are just guppies hoping to ride the right whale
yeah man, i totally feel that 100%

>Its why I never tell anyone to buy link, I know its a massive risk
we are on the same boat, and we think alike,it feels good to know there are still real linkies on biz, i stopped posting for years here because of the jeep spam and the timer they added with email validation and shit, they reduced the timer to 60 seconds so it kinda helps to post again, but i'm really glad to have spoken with you man thanks

>if the price moons the rewards will be small, potentially much smaller than current staking
that's because you are denominating the rewards in LINK, KEK, i just want to live off my staking rewards, so obviously if link pumps the rewards will be much higher in $ and less in LINk yeah i get you lmao

62316982
>>62316897
oh yeah i know, but i have a strategy, basically i hold on the rewards until 2029, i sold enough at literally 30$ (it was my target i set a limit order and it got filled, i didn't know it would be the top of this cycle ) to survive until 2029, i'll let my links staked until 2029 and i'll sell again when we're above 25 in 2029 to ensure i'll live to see 2034, so basically my strategy is to wait for the bull, sell the rewards there, (enough to hold 4 years) and i keep repeating that.

i have enough to hold till 2029 thanks to the build rewards also, i'm safe till 2029, i hope we go beyong 50$ in the next bull, but right now i don't need to worry about crypto at all, i don't give a fuck about the bear

62317017
I figured this out in 2022.
If link really was what it was shilled to be, it wouldnt be trading at $7 and sergey wouldnt be dumping millions at even $5, if sergey really believed in his project and had insider knowledge that it would vastly appreciate, he wouldnt be dumping so much. it goes against human nature.
Dumping to fund development is a lie, what is the $ amount of total dumped tokens at this point? $7 Billion dollars?
It doesnt cost anywhere near that to fund a small software company, and no one ever asks, where did all this money go?
If I had to guess the majority of it was embezzled into offshore bank accounts in typical Russian oligarch fashion. Sergey probably has a vast fortune complete with lambo collections and penthouses in the Maldives.
7 BILLION dollars...
If chainlink has 1000 employees, how much does he have to be paying them per year to burn through that much money....

62317065
>>62317017
i did the math before (chatgpt did with some guidelines)
basically running the company costs between 160 - 290 Million $ / year check pic related for explanation. and if you look at how many tokens they sell on average, it's almost 300M$ which is close to the higher estimate. so yeah they actually burn that money, also chatgpt didnt account for network subsidy and build incubator or smartcon n shit, also, chainlink made multiple aquisitions, and they never revealed how much they paid for those acquisitions, recently they bought town crier, deco and recently rhombus, they prolly paid millions of $ and they will probably make more acquisitions

62317093
>>62316655
>what about the new dead horse we're beating?
just two more years

62317097
>>62316876
>>62316939
thank you both for doing more FUD than us FUDchads ever could lmao

62317143
>>62316982
>>62316939
That's smart (your strategy of keeping afloat I mean), and I agree its sad how botted/jeeted this website has become. Not to mention psychos like adem that have to spam the board 24/7 (I'm shocked he hasn't popped into this thread yet).

Thing is, if they continue to dump at the same rate, its all going to be done by 2029/2030 so you'll know by then. At that point its sink or swim, if chainlink hasn't gotten off the ground with staking 1.0 by then, there's nothing left for them.

I do worry sometimes that other players are manipulating the price to sink this. I've seen people say Sergey's doing it but that makes no logical sense, if he could do that he'd just inflate it and make himself rich. To me there's two reasons for suppression, neutral actors who understand what's being built but that it isn't built yet, and their best long term strategy to accumulate is to keep the price down. And bad actors, who understand link is funding development, and by keeping the price down they can potentially sabotage link on a long enough timeline. The other is bad actors who aren't necessarily trying to replace link, but don't want to see it get retail interest because it fucks with their bottomline, such as CZ and anyone who only views the market as a casino.

62317153
>>62317097
we are linkies, we suffer and we know why we are suffering, the difference between us and any other bagholder is that we know what we are holding and we arn't deluded, we know risks, and the rewards, we've been holding for almost 9 years now, nothing will make us sell, not even reality

62317156
>>62315242
oh cool adem's here. serious question why do you live on this board 24/7? i and many other long time holders only post like once a week, or month even at this point. why do you do it?

62317182
>>62317143
you're very smart, because while i read you i have thoughts that come up, and you answer those thoughts. bene lurking 4chan since 2007 or some shit i've learnt over the years to spot smart / legit people, and you are one, it's rare to meet good people on 4chan back in early days of crypto we used to meet alot of people like you too bad now we only have dumb fucks like these guys
>>62317097
>>62315242
also yeah it's kinda cringe that they keep fudding for absolutely no reason for all these years i don't even see what their goal is, my goal is to make the cll team stress on twatters so they don't feel like we are happy with them, so they stfu and focus on working.
what's these guys goals? probablmy grifters from xrp or some other shitcoin

62317281
>>62313738
You pic is such a retard faggot argument. So what if you are right that they are suppressing chainlink? You’re still poor as fuck lmao.

FYI Xlm had no problem pumping on the exact same news chainlink had. Your coin is a piece of shit

62317289
>>62316251
So why are you buying chainlink you fucking retard lmao

item.title: Polymarket, scamming once again — 24 replies — 86.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polymarket, scamming once again

Open thread • Replies: 24 • Updated: 2026-06-04 22:22 UTC

OP

Chuds thought once again they could beat jeet and jewish CEO insiders betting against whales on their own game.
I hope this shit to be the final nail in the coffin for all poolymarket shills scam threads on this board.

YOU WILL NEVER WIN
YOU CAN'T WIN
POOLYMARKET IS A SCAM
How many times do we need to say it?

remember the most important thing
polymarket markets are not decided on facts but how big UMA whales wallets are, so if they say 2+2=5, that's a "fact"
UMA Shitcoin= voting power
GET IT?

>Zelensky's suit
>Israel-Hamas cease fire
>Venezuelan elections
this isn't the first and won't be the last time were everyone is screwed.

RIP Polyscam

Early replies (first 3)

62315655
whats polymarket? is that where cucks swap their wives? sounds like a big sausagefest desu

62315679
>RIP Polyscam
They do this every week and nobody cares

62315709
>>62315638
They can't keep getting away with it!!!
I threw $100 for "yes" just for fun but I knew it was impossible to wink $100k and also I was a retard but yeat I'm never trusting again on any regular bet after they fucked this entire board on the Zelensky's suit market

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62315720
>>62315638
Ameri fat thread

62315762
>>62315655
It's a gambling site, they used sports betting, cut a neovagina into it, and now you can bet on all the events, not just sports.

62315765
>>62315638
This is also why Kleros and Chainlink don't work

62315821
>>62315638
Lmao and the jeet ceo tries to justify polymarket existence by saying muh insight of the mass muh education. I thought that polymarket decided the result till I looked into further and it was actually decided by whale crypto vote

62315830
>>62315821
The thing is they don't even have to do this to make a profit since they collect a fee and aren't on the other side of the prediction

62315874
>>62315765
polymarket's system failed because there was greater financial incentive to fake results than to play honest, this is exactly what chainlink fixes

62315923
>>62315874
>Big nodes say it is no
>Be honest and say yes
>You are determined to be lying since you went against the consensus and lose your stake

62316038
>>62315923
>honest players fork
>big nodes funds don't transfer
>anyone actually wanting reliable data switches to new network
tell me you understand nothing about link without telling me

62316051
>>62316038
You can't fork a centralized data service anon.

62316080
>>62316051
link is not designed to be centralized retardnon

62316099
>>62316038
>Just fork it bro
It's been seven years and this is the best you can come up with?

62316108
>>62316099
lol. what prevents bitcoin miners from double spending?

62316125
>>62316108
This is admitting that Chainlink doesn't have a unique product if the only thing ensuring honesty is the risk of a fork because you are absolutely correct that that is fundamentally how Blockchains work

62316131
>>62315638
Saw no at 34¢ after first dispute but didn't buy
If you want to make money you have to keep your emotions at the doorsteps

62316139
>>62316125
how is it not unique, compared to bitcoin or ethereum, when it uses this mechanism to bring off-chain data on-chain?

62316419
WHY are polymarket and kalshi the new normie thing when they're all aware it's even more rigged than crypto? It fucking frustrates me so bad that's where all the stinkin liquidity is at now so fucking retarded.

62316688
>>62315638
Just use kalshi, polymarket is a scam site.

62317124
>>62316419
Its like the 99.9% playing against the 0.1% money, its the same with these shitcoin pumps like zec, hype and now fucking worldcoin, nobody is using these shits to anything and they just randomly did 2-3x in a few weeks whike the bug coins just dumped, so there is no new liq only cartel whales pumping 100x a coin with 99x their own money in to win that 1x retail goy money, since i am in cryoto, jews are seems ez af!

62317127
>>62317124
Big coins*

62317235
>>62316131
the only way to make money is betting on whales side even if something is false.
the house(insiders)always win.
FACTS don't matter

>>62316688
what's the difference? what's the "consensus" mechanism?
I refuse to believe that they're more transparent or better to Polymarket, otherwise Kalshi would be the #1 spot right now
can't tell if you're legit or just another shill

62317255
>>62315638
>gambling in a casino you don’t own
None of them were gonna make it anyway.

item.title: ZEC exploit sell — 17 replies — 68.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
ZEC exploit sell

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-06-04 22:34 UTC

OP

Active ongoing Zcash exploit right now, dumping fast

Early replies (first 3)

62317133
>>62317117
You know what is funny, this whole "exploit" is fake and gay, there was only positive bews in media about zec for months.... And its the same fake shit, now they will just reverse it and it will be 100% negative news for the jews pndd it, so they can shirt it down and when they turned off the fud button in the media they can reaccumukate it and zec will be the bestest thing gigahyoerbrutal muh ultraprivacy shitcoin again..... Jews are ez!

62317139
>>62317133
In short: exit liquidity hunting the goyim.

62317152
>>62317139
+1 they are pribably the same cartel behind the hype and world coin pnds too.... As i said jews are ez, dont play their game and stay safe out in the wilderness my fellow magalanians!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62317186
>>62317133
I mean they did this whole act the last fall, insane amount of paki shills here on biz and when they unloaded on the top, they just drooped in a random trans fag as ceo or something..... Its all about goyim milking....

62317197
Hope yall shorted kek

62317202
>>62317117
In the next 3-4 days zec will be the cancer of earth according to jewberg media and all your jewtube fagfluencers...... Screencap!

62317207
So there's literally no way of knowing if someone utilized the exploit before it was discovered and minted an unlimited amount of ZEC in the orchard pool? That's fucking hilarious

62317218
>>62317207
true, and this would be a solid proof that zec is truly anonymous and cryptographically correct

62317220
I spoonfed this shithole board with the ADA short and now this. Too bad There's not a single trader left here

62317223
>>62317218
Nope, it'd simply show that there were no ways to detect whether this 0d happened.

62317234
>>62317220
What are you talking about? ADA is dying, sell you bags while you still can. IRS won't let you write them off.

62317241
>>62317220
You are right, i am not a trader, but i bet wld is a bice short too, since its the bestest thing since sliced bread according to cryptinigga(paid nigger shill liquidating giyims for new ferraris)

62317252
>>62317220
>shorting Balaji
ngmi

62317259
>>62317117
zcash is the total israeli scam. It was almost dead 1 year ago or so, trading for 20$.i was in the telegram and bought some around 27$. i thought i would go to 100. then the mega pump happened. 100% not organic. And now we are learning there was an exploit that allowed people running nodes to print zec, and nobody will never know because of the privacy feature KEK.
That shit is beyond scam.

62317294
>>62317117
Zioncash, we always said it.

62317295
>>62317117
>Ztrannies cry inflation bug about Monero for years
>During peak Zcash shill mania get exposed as having an infinite mint exploit
O I am laffin

62317297
>>62317259
It was sooo annoying seeing the retarded shills make money with it last year. They were right, by price, thats whats so wrong with crypto.

item.title: Actual insider here. I think this needs some clarification because Link marines have been waiting for almost 9 years now… — 57 replies — 58.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Actual insider here. I think this needs some clarification because Link marines have been waiting for almost 9 years now…

Open thread • Replies: 57 • Updated: 2026-06-04 21:40 UTC

OP

Actual insider here. I think this needs some clarification because Link marines have been waiting for almost 9 years now and have been extremely patient. I can't really tell you too much because Chainlink Labs employees actually lurk biz (and post here) so they might able to know who I am. There has been an unexpected temporary delay of DTCC adoption due to the upgraded off-chain computation protocols (OCR 2.0) that had an unforeseen malfunction. However, the DONs seem to be fully online therefore the problem will be resolved quite rapidly.

In regards to SWIFT they actually seem to be fully operational by June. The main problem is that the network across EVM and non-EVM chains hasn't been fully tested to maximum capacity therefore releasing a product in such a short time would be quite a gamble for us. I'm confident enough that we'll be able to maintain a standardized interface to control the minimization protocol for the off-chain computation of the blockchain thus rendering the entire Chainlink ecosystem fully secured. At this rate I can only tell you that DTCC and SWIFT will be operational by the end of June, that's a certainty.

Strap yourselves in
It’s actually happening

Early replies (first 3)

62308409
AI post

62308412
>>62308361
>Actual insider here
Prove it

62308854
>>62308361
>wake up anon, new cope date just dropped
Thanks for the false hopium you beautiful larping nigger

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62309779
>>62309725
Do you understand what happens when payment abstraction gets switched on and a whole big bag of stables gets insta swapped for link? I’ll tell you. It means you get priced out, in seconds.

62309789
>>62309725
when i bought in 2019, never in my worst nightmares did i think the price would be $9 in 2026.

All news announcements and partnerships that doesn't effect price is equivalent to red hot torturers irons

62309813
>>62309789
Its because we're all living in hell and being tortured by elites for fun.

62309977
>>62308409
AI reply

62310043
>>62309141
what? you mean peepeepoopoo cuckold posts arent real fud?

62310082
>$8 dollarinos
stink will be worth $2 dollars at this rate by the end of june

62311031
>>62309292
how the fuck does the blockchain verify a pig's weight?

a weighing scales can do that. not the fucking blockchain

all the blockchain can do is save that information. but it can't verify that it reflects reality

62311046
>>62309779
Wen

62311473
>>62309779
Could you even imagine the salt of those selling at some point in the month before the flip is switched and it goes to $5,000 or something? I’m not even talking about 4chan people. I’m talking about some schmuck who just has like 300 Link for no reason other than he has a 50k portfolio with 8 different coins and has no emotional attachment to Link and never even thinks about it. And then bam… you just fucked yourself out of 1.5 million dollars. Thats way different than selling and it goes to $40 and then within 3 years it’s at $1,000 and missing out on a good chunk of change over a decent amount of time which is manageable.

62311642
>>62308361
I massively need this to enormously increase the price. But let's face it, it'll probably do fuck all, just like this >>62309779

Genuinely raging I didn't sell in 2021 as I would've been retired.

62311722
with every year, link marines bump the numbers they imagine the token will be to make up for all the lost time. it's too late for them, they will never sell, making their initial a donation to chainlink

62311909
>>62311642
Just 2 more years until Sergey lets us have the carrot he's been dangling in front of us. Hang tight!

62311939
>>62311909
The thing is that they’re building/developing and delivering. No one else comes close. If Link fails everything fails.

62311940
fuddies on suicide watch again

62311944
>>62311940
p + c, dumb stanker

62311996
>>62311939
They're delivering for CLL (the company), not the token holders. Token holders are thrown scraps in the form of the strategic reserve

62312010
>>62311944
c(ope) + s(needthe), detractor

62312018
>>62309779
>Payment abstraction gets switched on
>Stables get instant swapped for link...
>And then immediately get instant swapped for stables as soon as they land
You retards do realize this means nothing happens right? There's a reason people pay their AWS bills in USD and Bezos didn't decide to make his own Amazon Bucks to pay for the services in, right?

62312247
>>62312018
you do realize that it's discounted to pay in link?

62312338
>>62308854
At first it was for the pump and dump.
Nowadays it might be for senseless torture.
Or maybe some are testing some AI to see how much it is efficient at scamming people.

62313737
>>62312247
because it's the inferior payment method, have to advertise it somehow. yet everyone still pays with stables

62314528
>>62313737
wrong, it incentivizes link purchasing.

62314765
>>62309133
>>62309696
>>62309133
>carrot dangling

You do realize it's DTCC dangling this carrot, and not Sergey, right?

62314773
>>62313737
>everyone still pays with stables
You literally can't pay nodes with stables.

62314863
>>62308361
>>62308361
>>62308361
and there there is people like me who actually watch every peice of video on the internet about link.

and its funny that the only vidoes the yputube chainlink pulld down were the ones saying how far away everything in your post claims

but there used to be a 4 hour video of everyone clowning link, ncluding this dyke

62314878
>>62314863
How was she clowning link?

62315315
Why doesn't anyone ever lock their computers in these photos?

62315477
>>62308361
>There has been an unexpected temporary delay of DTCC adoption due to the upgraded off-chain computation protocols (OCR 2.0) that had an unforeseen malfunction.
It's odd that this would seemingly coincide with LINK's recent downgrade to an ERC-10 token by the ERC council. Coincidence?

62315489
>>62314878
Yeah bro Nadine was clowning on LINK when they are going live with them in 2 months and her daughter is interning there

Kek fuddies

62317098
>>62315489
nadine from dtcc likes Chainlink

item.title: Oil — 9 replies — 36.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Oil

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-06-04 22:34 UTC

OP

Anyone has experience with selling cooking/edible oil? I want to DIY a press and make sunflower oil with it, looks like a money hack.

Early replies (first 3)

62316133
>>62315908
I know a little about how it works in Europe. You can have a bunch of olive trees in your property and collect the olives any way you see fit, and then simply go with them to a certified dude with certified machinery that extracts the oil and gives it to you, charging some for the service.
In order to sell edible stuff you have to pass several controls, you cant just diy it. It would need to be certified machinery (stainless steel, food grade components...)
But if you are doing it in India or some shithole without laws go for it.

62316166
>>62315908
>and average sunflower contains 1,000-2,000 sunflower seeds
>when pressed, this produces on average 1-2 tablespoons of oil
Have fun i guess

62316223
the regulatory compliance is going to cost you infinity dollars

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62316343
Who do you think is gonna move all those seeds, bottle all that oil, screw the press and clean everything up after.

You dont have a robot. Fucking dumbass. That is a 3rd world setup.

62316386
>>62316343
>>62316133
in greece or in other balkan counties you can absolutely sell small amounts at local markets without any "checks"

62316398
>>62316386
Ok so you bottle up all the oil you pressed and then you take it to the farmers market and sit there all day for the entire week trying to sell it to all the people walking by.

Have fun making less than minimum wage.

62316530
>>62315908
The real money-hack here is to make your own biodiesel with lye, methanol, and oils. You can drive anywhere for free once you get a diesel engine. Hack the engine to bypass stupid pollution laws and you'll get 100mpg

62316616
>>62315908
Yes
>sneed oils

62317296
>>62316166
>150 tablespoons for like 20 bucks
the fuck, modern agriculture and capitalism is incredible, because i can't imagine what that shit would cost even 100 years ago

62316570
OP is lying scamming jeet, metc up 1% big gains huh faggot

62316579

62317195

62317216

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62313141
>>62313127
I remember the $100K day, when your hubris compelled you to push to $1M. You should have gracefully exited and become a part of 4chan and BTC history. Now you will suffer the pain of this struggle for the remainder of your life.

62314749
>>62313141
At that time there were several jeet contingents. Half were trying to get me to support their various snail shitcoins. The other half were trying to take over snail posting so they could later push their own shitcoins. I did what was necessary to keep the Bitcoin snail from being jeetified. But you probably know that.

62317173
>>62313127

item.title: *continues to predict the price of BTC in your path* All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're… — 119 replies — 62.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
*continues to predict the price of BTC in your path* All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're…

Open thread • Replies: 119 • Updated: 2026-06-04 20:44 UTC

OP

*continues to predict the price of BTC in your path*

All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're close to a buy signal now

prev thread:
>>62261213

Early replies (first 3)

62284885
How do I use this?

62284969
>>62284885
green = buy dips
red = sell rips
yellow = volatility likely, derisk
the site has a realtime FX-based confirmation layer - positive confirms buys and invalidates sells, negative confirms sells and invalidates buys
today FX is solidly green so early buys are defensible
chart gets updated every month with a new 4-week, next will be on Sunday

62284994
imagine buying bitcoin over 70K

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62305385
it's over

62306031
>>62305363
I had an asset do 200% in like 7 days recently, so it seems at least that people aren't turning up their noses at it all, so long as substantial developments are being made. Still not immune to dipping after considering profit-taking. Good projects can stand out in a storm like a lighthouse does. We'll see if that continues into the year.

62306711
>>62306031
Oh, I don't think crypto's over at all, unless someone has cracked SHA-256. Something is definitely happening to the asset class, though. A pro-crypto Fed chair just took over, stocks are up, but our internet coins are acting like they're about to be banned (for the tenth time)

62308784
Bump

62308863
>>62285441
You can trade at mexc and make maker orders close to the current price, that way only the taker pays 0.4-5% and you lose nothing.

62309487
>>62306031
Consistency is key.

62310796
>>62308784
I think we're fully in a news-driven cycle now, or maybe more accurately a institutional-crime-driven cycle. Physics-liquidity has no edge rn.
I'm working on this seasonality model which has caught some pivots so far, and if it's right we trend down to mid-June and then pump hard through the summer, but it's extremely experimental and I'll need months more results to compare to predictions before I could even start tuning it

62310807
>>62310796
it could also be the observer effect has broken your model

62310837
>>62310807
>the observer effect has broken your model
If Saylor's found my model he could at least send me a couple shekels
That said, about 95 times out of 100 a physics-predicted pivot that happens on a weekend gets frontrun the last trading day before, which does make me suspect institutions also figured this out

62310897
>>62285204
>the thing has a ~80% hit rate over more than a year

80% of the time it works all of the time

62310926
based

62311911
Bro..?

62313065
>>62311911
Boomer stocks keep doing what they're supposed to but crypto's getting killed = this isn't market-driven, we're getting fucked

62313095
>>62285468
Tell me how to day trade using the moon phases

62313156
I want to use this but I fear I am incredibly stupid. Also why give this out for free? Isn’t the number 1 advice to keep an edge for yourself if you find one? This seems like too good to be true

62313227
>>62313156
>Also why give this out for free? Isn’t the number 1 advice to keep an edge for yourself if you find one?
It's a multi trillion dollar asset class, or at least it used to be. We're not going to move it
>I want to use this but I fear I am incredibly stupid.
Buy on confirmed green/sell on confirmed red/derisk on confirmed yellow, there's live gauges plus confirmation/invalidation on the site, and set a SL so you don't get rekt like this week when the system signaled "Buy" and everything else went up but crypto got drowned in a lake

62313294
>he's still going
Fucking based.

>>62313065
>"boomer stocks"
AI stocks literally move as fast as crypto used to lmao. This board loves Hype (or maybe 'love' is a strong word, but the 'it's gonna be the next 100 billion crypto in 2027' posters are shilling it hard) but hype is only up 4x from the lows. Meanwhile SNDK was 230 bucks in December and is now 1800 bucks. SNXX, the LETF, went up 8x from launch. The Micron LETF was 50 bucks in december and 1000 bucks now- literally 50k in that would be 1m in 5 months.

62313308
>>62313227
sorry im a bit retarded here. so you're saying this bounce continues for a few days, then back down and then pump into summer?

62313309
>>62313227
What does volume reduction mean in your trade simulator? Also that simulator is still way in beta right? Cuz according to it lower SL is always better, 0% SL bringing the best results. And leverage what already talked about but damn I thought I could become buffet starting with my 500$ portfolio for a sec

62313323
>>62313294
>AI stocks literally move as fast as crypto used to lmao.
Sometimes I wish I was in the timeline where I rotated all my ETH into NVDA in 2021. I did enough that it doesn't look like I'll need a job again, but maybe it'd be nice to have my own island

>>62313309
>Also that simulator is still way in beta right? Cuz according to it lower SL is always better, 0% SL bringing the best results. And leverage what already talked about but damn I thought I could become buffet starting with my 500$ portfolio for a sec
Extremely in beta. I added all the options (leverage, SL etc) a week ago and it's clearly fucked up the SL math. Big update's coming mid-month and it'll be fixed by then at the latest
>What does volume reduction mean in your trade simulator?
It's for the derisk on a confirmed Volatility signal. The demo strategy, in the t just cuts to 50% cash. Ofc you could just move stops up or whatever suits your own trading style

62313334
>>62313323
How much are you gonna charge for it when it’s done and how long do you think that’ll take?

62313351
>>62313334
>How much are you gonna charge for it when it’s done and how long do you think that’ll take?
Nowhere near happy enough with the site to even think about charging money for it. I'll charge for API access and the trade bot when that's done, probably this summer. Maybe a subscription for a TG channel with specific price signals? Dunno, that's all way in the future

62313359
>>62313351
And "confirmed buy/sell" means that FXwave confirms the already marked signal on the given day if I’m understanding correctly?

62314730
As a hobby, I'm putting two to three thousand AUD (5 bucks) into Bitcoin and strictly following your indicator. We'll see how it goes, moonbro.

62315151
Last week buy signal sisters how are you doing?

62316250
>>62313359
Going to try reply #3 to this to see if the jannies have actually shadowbanned me. Basically: Yes.

>>62314730
Godspeed

>>62315151
>Last week buy signal sisters how are you doing?
s-still beating buy & hold

62316626
>>62316250
Sorry for asking so many questions but you gotta get I’m skeptical. If this actually worked it‘d change my life and everytime I’ve come across some magical trading strategy that I had hopes for I only lost money. Also no you’re not shadow banned I guess

62316716
>>62316626
>Also no you’re not shadow banned I guess
Yeah, tried to send two replies last night and both times nothing posted. Weird.
>Sorry for asking so many questions but you gotta get I’m skeptical.
I appreciate the questions anon, I love talking about it, and also I was mostly expecting to have to endure a firestorm of trolling after this week's massive L so a real conversation is nice lol
This board changed my life, freed me from waging so I can spend life playing around with my autistic science experiments, so I'm here to give something back

62316751
>>62285791
Not sure if you meant this to sound so profound, the moon indeed creates the tides just like the waves of buying and selling allow us to get to the moon. It's beautiful.

62316819
>>62316716
How has this board changed your life? The only good advice I got here is "buy bitcoin if it’s low" which I definitely should follow more but it alone wouldn’t give me "financial freedom"

62317225

item.title: No real Bitcoiner is getting shaken out by this. — 116 replies — 27.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
No real Bitcoiner is getting shaken out by this.

Open thread • Replies: 116 • Updated: 2026-06-04 18:24 UTC

OP

Saylor sells 32 BTC out of 843,000+ BTC and anon starts acting like Bitcoin is dead.

You're not a real BTC holder.

Early replies (first 3)

62299202
why did he sell 32 btc tho?

62299205
>>62299202
to get a higher rating from some corporate wagie on wall street. he was showing his btc holdings are unencumbered and can be sold at will.

62299325
Didn't he tell people to never sell?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62313253
>>62313157
If you're into privacy, monero is better than nerva

62313260
>>62313239
monero ser

62313269
>>62313260
will never be mainstream like BTC or ZEC are.

62313275
>>62313269
What's nerva

62313321
He has burned through 12 months of cash in 6 months. He will have 0 cash on hand in about two months at the current rate if he doesn't sell any bitcoin to offset it. What he will probably do is jackup the yield even more which means he will have to sell even more bitcoin each month to cover it (already $150M a month atm)

62313462
>>62313245
I meant the new TBV public testnet. It's basically a chance to try native BTC-backed borrowing through Aave V4 anon.

62313465
>>62303647
We never know their plans

62313476
>>62303658
I don't think anyone really knows how the market would react until it's actually tested.

62313530
>>62305074
His influence is hard to ignore, but I don't think one player completely defines the market.

62313567
>>62313530
What happens when every major holders sell their btc

62313591
>>62313462
If you hold btc, hold. Don't do anything risky. Defi is no longer safe.

62313595
>>62306852
NICE FUD

62313964
>>62313591
but I think the quality of the protocol matters a lot. Some projects go through multiple independent audits before launch, so I wouldn't put every DeFi protocol in the same category.

62313972
>>62313321
Maybe. I don't think it's as straightforward as that, but it'll be interesting to see how it plays out.

62313977
>>62307496
Market buys would have been a lot more entertaining, that's for sure.

62314023
>>62299824
Why do you say that?

62314048
>>62312315
Whether people like Saylor or not, Bitcoin itself doesn't really care who owns it.

62314204
>>62299187
He's about to slurp up the remaining 157k

62314317
Saylor sold a pittance of his bitcoins (32) to crash the market, now he's going to swoop in and buy even more, the man is playing 5D chess.

62314711
>>62312888
You're depending to this guy?

62314748
>>62313260
>monero
Absolute trash.

62315125
>>62313964
There's no more hype in there

62315140
>>62314748
Why ????

62315517
>>62313144
>Bitcoin is a global asset with millions of participants.
The combined order depth before bitcoin goes to literally 0 is less than 10,000 orders, many placed by the same person.
Bitcoin literally has less people actively participating than your local Supermarket.

62315586
>>62308048
oh nice, so Michael is fine, it's everybody else who trusted him with their monies who are getting utterly bobo'd to death? Cool, cool...

62316045
>>62315125
Maybe that's a good thing. Less hype and more focus on building infrastructure that gives assets something to do beyond just sitting in a wallet.

62316053
>>62314317
Maybe, but I doubt 32 BTC alone is moving a market this big.

62316059
>>62313253
I guess it depends on what you're looking for, but Monero has built a pretty strong reputation over the years.

62316066
>>62314048
Pretty much. The network treats everyone's coins the same.

62316193
Holy shit people are so low IQ.
>why did he sold?
>why does he keep buying btc
>how will he pay dividends?
>he is about to get liquidated
The cope will be unreal when coffezillafags realize they punched above their weight class here.

item.title: Reddit trannies are absolutely LIVID about heckin Muskerino and now are seething & coping about SpaceX IPO. — 20 replies — 13.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Reddit trannies are absolutely LIVID about heckin Muskerino and now are seething & coping about SpaceX IPO.

Open thread • Replies: 20 • Updated: 2026-06-04 21:07 UTC

OP

Reddit trannies are absolutely LIVID about heckin Muskerino and now are seething & coping about SpaceX IPO. They fucking hate this stock.

So, that basically means, it's a guaranteed 10x within a year, right?

I don't care about most of you fucking streetshitters, I'm buying it day 1, even if it dips immediately it's guaranteed to go to retarded heights and the orbit. Jannies ruined /biz/, a few years ago we would be all over this shit.

https://www.reddit.com/r/stocks/comments/1twsu5s/spacex_is_complete_garbage/

Early replies (first 3)

62315921
can we scam boomers by buying this? i heard they were forced to buy with their 401k? wtf is that shit lmao?

62315941
>>62315900
yeah bro its gonna 10x to 20 trillion

62315953
>>62315921
probably want to sell the initial squeeze...im not holding anything that doesnt pay a dividend after that shit

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62315957
>>62315941
>he still thinks there's anything rational about finance

62315985
>>62315900
Shill me the feasible use cases in a five to ten year horizon please.
If it has more utility than Hyundai's home and trades robots, I might buy after the initial overbuy panic sell.

62316070
>>62315900
It’s just "Elon bad", they would find it amazing if it were the space company of Bernie Sanders

62316079
>>62315900
their mod banned xer though

62316091
>>62315985
Orbital data centers and moon colony sar then asteroid minings. Immediate value from the xAI inteligence very profitable sar.

62316103
>>62316070
>It’s just "Elon bad",
this, but it actually has an effect on Elon's sales and ability to procure government contracts

62316120
>>62316070
no it's not you dumbass, read the post. They don't like the index fund scam

62316145
>>62316120
the fact that they consider it overprices indicates a huge change in public perception of musk and his projects.

a couple years back the left and center would have been cheering this as an amazing opportunity for every american and their retirement accounts

62316161
>>62315985
>use cases
lmao retard
stay poor

62316350
>>62316091
Sar don't buying this SpaceX scams is by nazi elon musk, kindly buy KASPA sar

62316355
>>62315900
The numbers are RIGHT THERE
>proceeds to ignore the numbers
Gradually I began to hate them.

62316408
>>62315900
>So, that basically means, it's a guaranteed 10x within a year, right?
Wow, 10x? Sounds REAL!
thank you concerned citizen!

62316410
>>62316091
utter bullshit.
THe only path to profitability is space war.

62316651
He is correct though, how is this all not a complete scam? They change basic rules that are there to protect funds from buying volatile stocks and loosing a lot of money just to obviously prop up a overvalued stock

Whats their reasoning here? That they would miss gains in the time they would normally wait? Top signal

62316766
>SHAME ON YOU Bank of America, Deutsche Bank, UBS, Citigroup, JPMorgan, Mizuho, RBC, Macquarie, Wells Fargo, Allen & Co, Needham, Raymond James, Stifel, Cantor Fitzgerald, Soc Gen, Mirae, Santander, ING, and BTG Pactual.
Whut. Dems love banker cock. Fighting tooth and nail to build an "anti-crypto army" and shutting down anything that provides any IOTA of competition to the banking cartel like stablecoin yield.

62316893
>>62315900
It will go up.
Don't ask why.
Because space and shit.
Just buy it.

62316917
It will double in market cap within ~2 years. It has room to run.

item.title: Germany is such a corrupt shithole — 31 replies — 7.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Germany is such a corrupt shithole

Open thread • Replies: 31 • Updated: 2026-06-04 18:21 UTC

OP

Now they are forcing UBER to almost double their prices because taxi drivers demanded it (since their prices were so expensive nobody would use them anymore). So now all we have is absurd UBER prices.

Early replies (first 3)

62308549
>>62308545
just walk u lazy krautnigger

62308551
>>62308545
>NOOOO this amerikanische company must make money off us and fleece its workers!!!

62308590
>>62308545
uber revolutionized taxis for me
before uber i was utterly loathe to take a taxi
>ring the taxi company
>background noise
>some mumbling arab on the phone
>spitting at you
>doesn't even appear to want your custom
>no idea what the price will be
>no idea how long it will take
>taxi driver is probably an illegal
but now it is consistently a great experience and simply how taxis should be
>put in my exact location and destination
>told exactly what it will cost
>told exactly where the driver is
>told exactly what vehicle they are in
>drivers have a rating so cars are always clean and they are always pleasant
>plus there are tips
>riders also have a rating and drivers are given discretion about who they want to take
i honestly struggle to think of any other industry which has been so much improved by a singular app
it's not good that uber has such a monopoly but taxi companies deserved this by being so shit that they couldn't compete
if uber really was just added cost then it wouldn't be competitive but not only is it more convenient and more pleasant it isn't even more expensive
i don't believe for one moment that it is actually worse for most drivers either because before they needed a taxi agency which undoubtedly siphoned their revenues whereas now they are essentially independent under uber

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62308626
>>62308549
I unironically do
>>62308551
YOU FUCKING RETARD PEOPLE LIKE YOU ARE THE PROBLEM
They were offering the same service for cheaper. CLEARLY they were making less money then the corrupt taxxi assholes. And muh poor workers, they earn their normale wage, give them extra if you want. If they would earn so little they would just ask for Bürgergeld (social welfare). So clearly they were the far more efficient service compared to that old bullshit taxi system.
>>62308590
Yes exactly and the german government hates on it since the very start and put out media campaigns against it, and now they are jsut trying to bruteforce UBER out.

62308629
>>62308590
>i don't believe for one moment that it is actually worse for most drivers either because before they needed a taxi agency which undoubtedly siphoned their revenues whereas now they are essentially independent under uber
Oh yeah this system is max extraction, and the government takes massive share with licences and shit.

62308644
>>62308629
German here. take as much as you can from the gov and give them nothing. dont work (schwarzarbeit is ok). Stop saving/using euro so inflation tax wont work. That way we can actually make a difference by not giving this hostile shit government money.

62308751
>>62308590
lel the cattle ladies and gentlemen
>>62308629
you VILL pay for the uber license

62308768
>>62308551
If Americans making money is the problem, then ban UBER but allow equivalent European alternatives

The reason drivers are "fleeced" is because the there's an abundance of people who can drive a car, so drivers have little negotiating leverage.

62308823
>>62308768
>abundance of people who can drive a car
allowed to yes, can no

62311555
>>62308626
>If they would earn so little they would just ask for Bürgergeld (social welfare).
You think the illegals aren't leeching off welfare? Lol

>>62308768
>so drivers have little negotiating leverage.
They have little negotiating leverage since Uber claims to just be a middle man linking you to an independent driver that has to get paid through them. That way they can circumvent some fees, taxes and social security.

62312332
>>62311555
Checked. So they dont actually get fleeced if they collect welfare aswell. Only the government. Which is not my problem.

62314310
>>62308626
Oh no, you have to accept it when a big hostile company uses shareholder money to crush all the competition via price dumping, hires illegal workers from India who do not pay taxes, and then when all the competition has given up increases the prices in its newly created monopoly in something as innovative as a taxi service.

Only smart people can run a taxi service you must accept our smart American (((innovation)))

62314337
>>62308768
There is also an abundance of people who can operate an app like Uber.

The thing is that Uber uses stakeholder money to crush the competition and to create a monopoly.

No productive value is generated by this kind of capitalism.

Also, a lot of people in Germany who work for Uber or Delivery Hero are working illegally and were scammed into debt by being promised a job at a big German company and then they have to work gigs 12 hours a day. It is basically like slavery.

62314512
>>62308545
If you run a restaurant and have to follow all the restaurant regulations but then someone create a app and have random people just cooking in their kitchen with no food safety i think you would have a problem with that as well
Even if you might disagree with some of the regulations
Taxi drivers have to follow certain rules and regulations and it's part of why the price is higher and they don't want to be undercut by uber

62314906
>>62314512
I dont have any problem with streetfood. In fact i dont go to german restaurants anymore because of the absurd prices but go gladly to tropical countries and eat streetfood. And with food it even makes more sense. With taxis its just literal extortion. And please dont tell me that the arab taxi drivers are good at driving.

62314914
>>62308545
Abduallah needs more money for his children chud.

62314921
>>62314310
Then regulate UBER more and force competition if you want. But dont force high prices. And not paying taxes, i dont see the problem.

62315352
>>62312332
Fleeced by uber, they don't know whether their drivers, I'm sorry their independent contractors, are on any welfare programs.

62315380
>>62308626
i don't know exactly about germany, but i suppose taxi drivers have to pay some license and taxes uber fags don't.
That's the case in many countries. So enjoy ur socialism mehmet

62315579
>>62308545
That's not corrupt you seehing urdie, european counires have regulated taxis hat meet safety standards. They don;t need rapey jeets in unsafe cars making profits for some does not give a fuck american app corporate that could no give a shit and acts to create black market work for immigrants who are in Europe illegally.

62315590
>>62314921
If uber wants to open taxi companies in europe they are free to, they don't, they want unsafe cars and rapey third world illegal drivers for profit. uber can go fuck itself.

62315619
>>62314921
>And not paying taxes, i dont see the problem.
Fuck off back to brownland you'd have our hand out for social security paid for by europeans taxes fast enough you third world vermin. Hop in your uninsured uber with defecive brakes and piss off back to india, pakistan, nigeria whereever to face your gangrape trial. Europe does not need or want you or fucking uber faciliaing illegals and their scrapyard death rap cars driven by rapists and child molestors

62315739
>>62308751
>implying taxis don't need a license anyway
>implying anyone would book your taxi if you weren't on a popular app
Faggot retard.

62315793
>>62314310
>>62314337
Taxi companies use illegal labor too don't bullshit me and say that they don't. You're painting some picture of poor white german taxi drivers but we all know they were arabs and jeets before this anyway bringing over their relatives.
I have more faith in uber to do background checks and at least make sure the driver has a fucking license than muhammad muhammad.

If there is some rampant issue with uber drivers being uninsured and driving shitboxes (there isn't) then people can report them and indict uber.
>oh no they undercut the other apps
Ie the other apps can't compete and neither in price nor in quality.
There isn't room in the market for multiple taxi apps. People will install one and that's the one they will use. "uber" has already replaced the word "taxi" for many people so it's been decided. They should perhaps let drivers set their own rates to create internal competition but it would have to be tiered.

62315862
>>62308545
german flag is so beautiful when its upside down
>golden clouds
>red horizon
>black land covered in the shade of twilight
hte original was upside down too

62315889
>>62308545
Why should Kike companies be allowed to generate profits in other countries?

It's about time US Kike corporations are bitch slapped around the world.

62315938
>>62315862
Show me the real German flag

62316037
>>62315619
See >>62308644
this here
The government uses the tax you pay to import the people you talk about. So forget your loyality to your state.
>>62315590
>taxi companies
Thats the problem. The moment you have these the state extraction starts. We want cheap transportation not taxi companies.

62316046
>>62315579
>regulated taxis hat meet safety standards
Yeah haha lol. What exactly needs to be regulated? The drivers are browns anyway.

62316068
>>62308545
wait, they're protecting GERMAN workers at the expense of some US corporation trying to undercut prices?

how dare they? Let those Germans starve if they can't compete with every poor person in the world!

kek. This is why the US doesn't allow chinese corporations to take over US services. Could you imagine what wages would drop to?

62316170
>>62315938
Here you are
